reforms.description: The government plans to increase expenditures in information technology and science and technology by about 16%, aiming to boost R&D outlays in the central government budget from 4.1% to 4.4%. This is part of a broader objective to reach 5% by 2003.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing R&D funding is expected to enhance innovation and productivity in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The increase in R&D expenditures has been confirmed as part of the government's budgetary actions.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, the increase in funding is part of a broader strategy rather than a standalone major reform.
reforms.source_quote: Expenditures in the categories of information technology and science and technology are both expected to rise by about 16%.

reforms.description: The R&D tax credit was generalised by shifting from a positive list to a negative list system, which now excludes only a few industries, thereby broadening the corporate tax base and enhancing R&D incentives across various sectors.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Broader R&D tax incentives are likely to stimulate private sector investment in research and development.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been enacted and is currently in effect, providing tax incentives to firms.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant overhaul of the R&D tax credit system, impacting a wide range of industries.
reforms.source_quote: The R&D tax credit was generalised by shifting from a positive list to a negative list system that excludes just a few industries.

reforms.description: The number of government programmes to support small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) quadrupled from around twenty in the early 1990s to 80 in 2001, with total expenditures reaching 5 trillion won, equivalent to 1% of GDP. Additionally, the government shifted from full to partial guarantees for loans to SMEs, enhancing the screening and management functions of creditor banks.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Expanding support for SMEs is expected to foster innovation and economic growth by enhancing access to resources and financing.
reforms.status_evidence: The expansion of SME programmes and the new loan guarantee system have been confirmed as operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The significant increase in SME support programmes and the reform of loan guarantees represent a major shift in policy.
reforms.source_quote: This year, it completed a shift begun in 1999 from full to partial guarantees, which range from 70 to 85 per cent of the loan amount.
